  • Plotting: pl

Plotting: pl#

The plotting namespace provides standalone Matplotlib panels and combined summary figures. Most functions accept ax=None for standalone plotting and save=None for optional file output.

Drift-field plots#

scjdo.pl.summary_figure(adata, key='scjdo', basis='X_pca', save=None)[source]#

Assemble the four-panel Figure 3 layout: (a) drift field, (b) local sensitivity, (c) archetypes, (d) coordination.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • basis (str)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.drift_field(adata, key='scjdo', basis='X_pca', color='pseudotime', velocity_key=None, stream=True, stream_density=1.2, n_grid=30, min_cells=3, ax=None, save=None, **scatter_kw)[source]#

Plot drift field on the specified embedding.

By default uses streamlines (stream=True), which integrate the flow continuously and make bifurcations visible even when the 2D projection captures only a fraction of the total velocity energy.

Parameters:

  • basis ('X_pca' or 'X_umap'.)

  • color ('pseudotime' (default) or any column in adata.obs.)

  • velocity_key (Velocity to visualize. Auto-selects 'X_velocity_pseudo') – (pseudotime gradient, always coherent) when basis=’X_pca’, or ‘X_drift’ (full model drift) otherwise.

  • stream (Use streamplot (True, default) or quiver arrows (False).)

  • stream_density (Streamline density passed to matplotlib.streamplot.)

  • n_grid (Grid resolution for velocity interpolation.)

  • key (str)

  • min_cells (int)

  • ax (matplotlib.pyplot.Axes | None)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.sensitivity(adata, key='scjdo', ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Plot max real eigenvalue (local sensitivity) across pseudotime.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • ax (matplotlib.pyplot.Axes | None)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.archetypes(adata, key='scjdo', ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Plot archetype temporal activation profiles.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • ax (matplotlib.pyplot.Axes | None)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.coordination(adata, key='scjdo', axes=None, save=None)[source]#

Plot archetype coordination: correlation heatmap + activation timeline.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • axes (tuple | None)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.instability_genes(adata, key='scjdo', n_genes=10, sensitivity_threshold=0.05, per_archetype=True, save=None)[source]#

Plot and tabulate the top genes driving local instability across pseudotime.

For each sensitive pseudotime window (Re(λ_max) > sensitivity_threshold), the eigenvector associated with the maximum real eigenvalue is projected to gene space via PCA loadings. Genes with the highest loadings on this “unstable direction” are those whose perturbation is most amplified by the drift field at that point in the trajectory.

Parameters:

  • n_genes (Number of top genes to show in plots and table.)

  • sensitivity_threshold (Min Re(λ_max) to consider a window sensitive.)

  • per_archetype (Also show top instability genes per archetype.)

  • key (str)

  • save (str | None)

Returns:

table –

Columns: gene, mean_instability_score, peak_pseudotime,

primary_archetype, [archetype activation at peak]

Return type:

pandas.DataFrame

Bridge plots#

scjdo.pl.bridge_source_target(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', basis='X_pca', ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Scatter: source vs target vs transit cells.

scjdo.pl.bridge_trajectories(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', basis='X_pca', direction='both', n_show=30, color='pseudotime', ax=None, save=None)[source]#

PCA scatter colored by pseudotime with forward and/or backward bridge trajectories overlaid as path lines.

Parameters:

  • direction ('forward', 'backward', or 'both'.)

  • n_show (Number of trajectory paths to draw (subset for clarity).)

  • color ('pseudotime' or any column in adata.obs for cell coloring.)

  • key (str)

  • basis (str)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.bridge_instability(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Instability curves: forward vs backward + asymmetry.

scjdo.pl.bridge_archetypes(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', axes=None, save=None)[source]#

Archetype activation profiles for both directions.

scjdo.pl.bridge_genes(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', n_genes=15, axes=None, save=None)[source]#

Gene × archetype heatmaps for forward and backward.

scjdo.pl.bridge_summary(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', basis='X_pca', n_genes=12, save=None)[source]#

Seven-panel summary figure for the Schrödinger Bridge analysis:

  1. PCA + forward trajectories

  2. PCA + backward trajectories

  3. Instability curves forward vs backward

  4. Instability asymmetry

  5. Training convergence

  6. Forward archetypes

  7. Backward archetypes

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • basis (str)

  • n_genes (int)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.bridge_gene_comparison(adata, key='scjdo_bridge', n_genes=15, save=None)[source]#

Two-panel gene heatmap (forward | backward) plus a Venn-style summary of shared vs unique instability drivers.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • n_genes (int)

  • save (str | None)

Regulator plots#

scjdo.pl.regulator_barplot(adata, key='scjdo_regulators', direction=None, n_show=20, ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Horizontal bar chart of top regulators.

Bar length = weighted_score (primary rank). Bar color = mean_instability (darker = higher quality targets). Dot = n_targets (secondary axis).

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • direction (str | None)

  • n_show (int)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.regulator_heatmap(adata, key='scjdo_regulators', scjdo_key=None, direction=None, n_show=15, ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Heatmap: rows = top TFs, columns = archetypes, values = mean instability of TF targets when that archetype is active.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • scjdo_key (str | None)

  • direction (str | None)

  • n_show (int)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.regulator_scatter(adata, key='scjdo_regulators', direction=None, n_label=10, ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Scatter: n_targets (X) vs mean_instability (Y). Dot size = weighted_score. Dot color = peak archetype. Reveals whether top regulators earn their rank through breadth or quality.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • direction (str | None)

  • n_label (int)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.regulator_profiles(adata, key='scjdo_regulators', scjdo_key=None, direction=None, n_tfs=3, ax=None, save=None)[source]#

For each of the top n_tfs regulators: line plot of their top target genes’ instability scores across pseudotime, plus a shaded band for archetype activation.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • scjdo_key (str | None)

  • direction (str | None)

  • n_tfs (int)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.regulator_network(adata, key='scjdo_regulators', scjdo_key=None, direction=None, n_tfs=5, n_targets=6, n_denovo=4, ax=None, save=None)[source]#

Hybrid regulatory network graph.

Solid edges = reference database confirms TF → target AND target is a top

instability gene (known biology, validated by scJDO).

Dashed edges = de novo co-instability edges inferred from Jacobian eigenvector

co-loading — not in any reference database (novel finding).

Node color: TF nodes by peak archetype; target nodes by instability score. Node size: TF by weighted_score; target by instability score.

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • scjdo_key (str | None)

  • direction (str | None)

  • n_tfs (int)

  • n_targets (int)

  • n_denovo (int)

  • save (str | None)

scjdo.pl.regulator_summary(adata, key='scjdo_regulators', scjdo_key=None, direction=None, n_show=15, save=None)[source]#

Four-panel summary figure: (a) Ranked bar chart (b) TF × Archetype heatmap (c) Quality vs quantity (d) Target instability profiles (top 3 TFs)

Parameters:

  • key (str)

  • scjdo_key (str | None)

  • direction (str | None)

  • n_show (int)

  • save (str | None)
